specific-instructions.name: Project Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/instructions" and put the instructions.

header.name: description
header.hint: A short description of the project that will be used on the instructions template.

header.name: applyTo
header.hint: A short description of what the instructions apply to.

body.name: mainInstructions
body.description: The main instructions for the project. This section can be used on the instructions template to provide detailed guidance and context for the project.

body.name: repositoryName
body.description: The name of the repository where the instructions will be used. This variable can be used on the instructions template to customize the instructions based on the repository name.

specific-instructions.name: Context Instructions

custom.name: Context
custom.description: Prefix of the file name that will contain the context instructions. Use this value instead of the default context instructions file.

specific-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/instructions" and put the instructions. You need a main file with the name "copilot-instructions.md".

agent-instructions.name: Agent

custom.name: Context
custom.description: Prefix of the file name to replace the agent name. Use this value instead of the default agent name.

agent-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/agents" and put the agent file.

header.name: description
header.hint: A short description of the agent that will be used on the agent template.

header.name: argument-hint:
header.hint: The inputs this agent expects, e.g., "a task to implement" or "a question to answer".

header.name: tools
header.hint: specify the tools this agent can use. If not set, all enabled tools are allowed.

header.name: mcp-servers
header.hint: The MCPs online or local that only this agent will have access to. Must be in yml format.

body.name: role
body.hint: Describe the role of the agent in second person. This is important to set the right context for the agent and guide its behavior. For example, "You are a senior developer with expertise in React and Node.js" or "You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about the project".

body.name: Repository Name
body.description: The name of the repository where the agent will be used. This variable can be used on the agent template to customize the agent based on the repository name.

skills.name: Skill
skills.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/skills/[skill_name]/SKILL.md" and put the skill file.

prompts.title: Prompt

custom.name: Context
custom.description: Prefix of the file name to replace the prompt name. Use this value instead of the default prompt name.

prompts.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/prompts/[prompt_name]/PROMPT.md" and put the prompt file.

specific-instructions.name: Agent
specific-instructions.installation: Check your tools documentation for installation instructions. Mostly is just put on the project root.

skills.name: Skill
skills.installation: Check your tools documentation for installation instructions. Mostly is just put on the project root.

specific-instructions.title: Repository Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: On Firebase Studio or Gemini CLI, put the instructions file in the root.

agent-instructions.title: Assistant
agent-instructions.installation: On your project using ChatGPT, go to Assistants page, click on Create and paste your file content on "System instructions" box.

specific-instructions.title: Codex Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: On Codex, open Settings, on General, paste the file content on the "Personal Instructions" field.

agent-instructions.name: Agent
agent-instructions.installation: On your Agent Zero instance, create a folder with your agent name and paste this file inside.
